subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The exploration is coherent and logically developed from context through modelling to interpretation.
Clear introduction, body and conclusion with effective section headings guide the reader.
Material is concise and almost entirely relevant, with only very minor repetition.
weaknesses: Minor layout issues (line-breaks inside equations, misplaced footnote symbols) interrupt polish.
A few algebraic transitions are slightly rushed and could be broken into clearer steps.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the exploration shows a few minor lapses in formatting and occasional rushed algebra, it fully meets the Band 4 descriptors for logical development, structure and relevance. The work is exceptionally well organized, highly coherent and concise, justifying the top mark in Presentation.

strengths: Mathematical notation and calculus symbols are used consistently and correctly for the most part.
Multiple representations (diagrams, algebraic development, verbal explanation) complement each other effectively.
Derivations follow a clear logical flow from assumptions through to differential equation and solution.
weaknesses: Occasional notation slips (HTML subscripts, typos in exponents) and missing axis labels reduce precision.
Key algebraic manipulations (sign change in integrals, constant evaluation) are sometimes too brief.
Formatting inconsistencies (extra commas, unlabeled figures) hinder full clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The communication is good overall, with mostly consistent notation, effective use of representations and clear logical structure. Occasional lapses in labelling, notation and intermediate explanations keep it at Band 3 rather than Band 4.

strengths: The choice of a personal basketball drill and realistic court dimensions shows genuine engagement.
The modelling assumptions and use of GeoGebra reflect significant independent thinking.
Contextual interpretation of the parameter Î² demonstrates a personal approach.
weaknesses: The approach follows standard calculus techniques without highly innovative extensions.
Creativity is moderate; alternative strategies (e.g. optimisation, acceleration phases) are not explored.
Initiative is clear but not sustained into more advanced or novel investigations.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work exhibits clear personal engagement and independent thinking, fitting Band 2. However, it does not reach the level of outstanding or highly innovative approach required for the top band.

strengths: Reflection on the realism of Î² and its tactical implications is meaningful.
Thoughtful analysis compares model results to real-world play and suggests future lead-pursuit studies.
Outcomes are clearly evaluated, with limitations and future directions noted.
weaknesses: Reflection is relatively brief and lacks deeper quantitative sensitivity or error analysis.
Critical analysis remains at a descriptive level without exploring alternative assumptions in detail.
Comprehensive evaluation with future implications is touched on but not fully developed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and thoughtful analysis of results, consistent with Band 2. The reflection is not yet substantial or critically deep enough to merit Band 3.

strengths: Substantial and varied calculus (differential equations, logarithmic integration, arc-length) is applied expertly.
All mathematics directly addresses the aim with thorough understanding and highly accurate derivations.
Advanced techniques (coordinate shifts, implicit GeoGebra solutions) are used comprehensively and with insight.
weaknesses: A few algebraic typographical slips (e.g., in Î² definition) occur but do not affect correctness.
Minor leaps in logarithmic manipulations and sign changes could be better annotated for completeness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematical content is comprehensive, flawlessly integrated into the modelling context and shows expert application of course-level calculus methods. Minor superficial slips do not detract from the overall excellence, justifying the top mark for Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Within each derivation the logical flow is clear: assumptions â equations â elimination of T â differential equation â integration.  Occasional jumps (e.g. the constant evaluation step) require the reader to fill in work, keeping the communication at a âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ level.

Derivations are largely correct and steps are justified.  Numerical substitution and integration constants are handled well; a couple of algebraic typos (e.g., Î² definition line) have negligible impact on conclusions.  Thus understanding is thorough and accuracy high, yet not flawless enough for band 6.

The student employs diagrams of the court, vector arrows, a family of curves graph, algebraic working and verbal explanation.  These representations complement one another and are appropriate; however, some figures lack numerical scales and axis labels, so the usage is effective but not âhighly effectiveâ.

The argument progresses from a clearly stated personal context to modelling assumptions, derivation of the differential equation, solution and interpretation.  Each step follows logically and the reader can follow the chain of reasoning without major gaps, although a few algebraic transitions (e.g. sign change after applying the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus) are rushed.  Overall the work is coherent and logical but not polished enough for the highest descriptor.

Initiative is shown in sourcing dimensions, employing GeoGebra to solve an implicit equation and proposing future study of lead pursuit; creativity is moderate, not exceptional. Hence a solid but not top-level score.

The student analyses results by comparing Î² values, commenting on realism and linking back to defensive tactics.  The analysis is thoughtful yet not deeply mathematical (e.g., no sensitivity or error analysis), meeting the âthoughtfulâ descriptor.

Outcomes are evaluated against real-world play and limitations are acknowledged; future investigation into lead-pursuit is suggested.  While clear, the evaluation lacks the comprehensive depth (alternative data, quantitative error bounds) required for the highest band.

Reflection appears in section 4 where assumptions are reconsidered and the practicality of Î² â 0.699 is assessed.  The discussion is meaningful but relatively brief, so it fits âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ rather than âsubstantial critical reflectionâ.

Mathematical symbols, indices and calculus notation are mostly correct and used consistently (e.g. dy/dx, definite integrals, Î²).  Isolated slips such as HTML subscripts (v_o) and typos in exponents do not seriously hinder comprehension, so the work fits the âmostly consistentâ rather than the fully âaccurate throughoutâ level.

The study is clearly the studentâs own: they select realistic court dimensions, decide on simplifying assumptions and interpret Î² in the context of team speed.  The approach is personal but not highly innovative, fitting the middle band.

Most content directly supports the aim; irrelevant digressions are rare.  Some repetition occurs when the student re-states geometric justification and when constants are recalculated, so the exploration cannot be called âhighly conciseâ, yet it remains largely focused and economical.

Substantial calculus (arc length, separable differential equation, logarithmic integration) is wholly relevant to determining the defenderâs path.  All mathematics directly addresses the aim and is applied in context, meeting the descriptor of ârelevant mathematics used comprehensivelyâ; only minor slips prevent an âexpertâ rating.

Choosing a personal basketball drill and linking it to pursuit-curve theory shows significant independent thinking; the student derives the model rather than citing a ready-made formula.  Nonetheless the exploration follows standard calculus techniques and does not extend into alternative strategies or optimisation, so evidence stops short of âoutstandingâ.

A clear introduction/body/conclusion structure is evident and section headings guide the reader. Figures are placed close to the discussion and referenced.  Minor layout issues (line-breaks inside equations, footnote-like symbols in the wrong place) prevent the piece from feeling âexceptionally well organisedâ, but it is certainly âwell organised with clear structureâ.

Techniquesâcalculus of pursuit curves, modelling with dimensioned data, solving for parameters numericallyâare fully commensurate with HL level and applied effectively.  The work does not extend into higher-level differential-equation theory or numerical methods beyond GeoGebra, so band 5 is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The algebraic trick in equation (4) would benefit from a brief note explaining why multiplying numerator and denominator is valid here.
The integrated result \ln|secÎ¸+tanÎ¸| is handled correctly, reinforcing strong use of integration techniques.
Equation (5) clearly combines both sides and shows the constant C; well presented for solving boundary conditions.
The title clearly conveys the focus on applying pursuit curves to basketball, engaging the reader from the outset.
Consider adding the studentâs name, candidate number, and date to the title page for complete identification and compliance.
The personal background description demonstrates genuine engagement and situates the exploration in a real context.
Figure 1 provides a helpful visual but should include court dimensions or a scale bar for clearer quantitative reference.
Section headings in the introduction guide the reader effectively, showing clear organization of content.
The aim statement is clearly articulated, directly linking the pursuit-curve model to the exploratory question.
It would strengthen the aim to mention how the parameter Î²âs realistic range will be assessed in context.
The assumption of constant speed is justified, but consider briefly discussing how small acceleration phases might affect Î².
The ratio Î² is defined correctly, but use consistent LaTeX without HTML tags for subscripts to improve notation clarity.
Figure 3 effectively visualizes the new reference frame and reinforces the text explanation.
Assuming a straight path for the offensive player simplifies the model, though note potential deviations due to dribbling in the reflection.
The shift in reference plane is well explained, demonstrating clarity in choosing a simpler coordinate system.
Figure 2 lacks axis labels and units, making it harder to interpret the geometric relationships quantitatively.
The Geogebra-derived coordinates are detailed, but formatting typos (e.g., extra commas) could confuse the reader.
Splitting the long descriptive paragraph into shorter units would enhance readability and focus.
The sign change after swapping limits during differentiation is rushed; include an intermediate explanation of why the integral flips sign.
The use of FTC is correctly cited in equation (3), demonstrating clear mathematical communication.
Equation (1) is correctly derived but could benefit from clearer grouping of terms in the denominator.
The statement of the arc-length formula is accurate and shows good application of calculus tools.
Equations (8) and (9) are clearly presented and correctly labelled, aiding the logical flow.
The product rule derivation in the multi-line block omits a brief note on sign distribution; adding this would improve clarity.
Equation (4) cleanly captures the core differential equation in terms of Î², demonstrating strong model relevance.
The substitution to define Î± simplifies the algebra elegantly and makes subsequent steps easier to follow.
Pointing out that dÂ²y/dxÂ² is the derivative of dy/dx is trivial; consider omitting or consolidating this for conciseness.
The separation to obtain a solvable integral is executed effectively, showing good use of mathematical methods.
The technique of substituting \tanÎ¸ into the integral is well chosen and clearly justified.
Combining logarithms in equation (10) is correct but showing the log-rule steps explicitly would aid reader understanding.
The calculation of the constant C involves a jump in log arithmetic; include the intermediate log steps for full clarity.
The proof that the expression inside the log is positive is rigorous and well communicated.
The algebra from the log form to the separable expression is complex; add a brief rationale for each manipulation.
The integration to obtain y is correctly set up, demonstrating appropriate use of the power rule.
Calculating C after integration is lengthy; consider summarizing intermediate results to improve conciseness.
The closed-form for dy/dx is presented neatly, showcasing strong mastery of the differential solution.
The final general form (equation 8) is clearly stated and ready for graphical exploration of Î².
Figure 4âs family of curves is insightful but would benefit from axis labels and a legend explaining color coding.
Interpreting Î² = 0.699 in realâworld terms shows thoughtful evaluation of model results.
Relying solely on GeoGebra to solve for Î² is practical, but outlining any manual approach or sensitivity check would deepen analysis.
The proposal to explore lead pursuit methods demonstrates insightful reflection and clear future direction.
